raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "California grows more than 400 food and fiber products.",
    "California contributes to one third of the country's vegetables and two thirds of the nation's fruits and nuts.",
    "Ninety-nine point nine percent of certain commodities, including peaches, artichokes, olives, pistachios, almonds, and walnuts, are grown in California.",
    "California leads the nation in the supply of 75 different foods.",
    "Growing food successfully depends on factors such as weather changes, water allocations, labor availability, and trade stability.",
    "Unpredictable weather events can result in unexpected crop losses.",
    "Burdensome governmental policies and regulations negatively impact farmers.",
    "Stringent labor policies and wages put California farmers at a competitive disadvantage compared to other states and countries.",
    "Overly strict labor policies have led to the closure of many small farms and businesses in California.",
    "There is a contrast between the progress of the federal administration regarding food security and the actions of the California state leadership.",
    "Actions taken by the federal government, such as restoring water to California's agricultural regions, can influence food security.",
    "Lawsuits related to resource allocation can disrupt local agricultural productivity and food supply.",
    "California's Mediterranean climate and class one soil render it one of five prime regions for food production in the world."
  ]
}
```
